data.title: Who Stole The KitKat Bars? Dogecoin Says 'Woof,' Pengu Admits Nothing, Domino's Declares Culinary War, And Nobody's Taking A Break From The Jokes
data.description: Enjoying amusement at another person's expense might be considered inappropriate on social media, but major companies and cryptocurrencies couldn't resist joking about the recent KitKat chocolate heist. A staggering twelve tons of Nestle S.A.'s KitKat chocolate bars vanished in Europe when thieves hijacked the truck transporting them. The incident has sparked widespread laughter. Dogecoin, the leading meme-inspired cryptocurrency, responded with a playful "woof" as its official statement, embracing the spirit of the Shiba Inu. Similarly, NFT brand Pudgy Penguins shared a chocolate-stained photo of their mascot PENGU, humorously asserting its innocence in the theft. This playful banter has captivated many online users. The full account is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: TomTom selected by LOCUS to provide high-precision traffic data for location intelligence platform
data.description: In Amsterdam, the Netherlands, on March 31, 2026, TomTom (TOM2), a leading mapping and location technology company, revealed a collaboration with LOCUS, a company specializing in mobility analytics and location intelligence. This partnership will involve the incorporation of TomTom Traffic data into the LOCUS platform.

data.title: Trump Said Willing To End Iran War Without Strait Of Hormuz Reopening; Market Reacts
data.description: According to reports, President Donald Trump has informed his advisors that he is open to concluding the conflict with Iran, even if the vital Strait of Hormuz continues to be obstructed. This development was highlighted in a recent article by the Wall Street Journal. The news of Trump's stance on potentially ending the Iran war without the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz has had an impact on the market, as indicated in an article by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Marriott International and Lefay Announce Milestone Deal to Grow Luxury Wellness Offerings Globally
data.description: In a recent announcement, Marriott International and the Leali family, creators of Lefay, revealed their collaboration to introduce the esteemed luxury wellness brand Lefay into Marriott's offerings through a joint venture. This partnership marks a significant milestone as Lefay becomes the inaugural brand outside of Marriott's existing portfolio to join forces with the renowned hospitality group.

data.title: Hacker charged in $53M Uranium Finance hack, faces up to 30 years in prison for fraud and money laundering.
data.description: The indictment linked to Uranium Finance could result in a maximum prison sentence of 30 years for charges related to fraud and money laundering.

data.title: Rakuten Mobile Extends Partnership with Netcracker for AI-Enabled Digital BSS to Support its Mobile Network in Japan
data.description: A collaboration between Rakuten Mobile and Netcracker has been expanded in Waltham, Massachusetts. The partnership aims to implement a cutting-edge Digital Business Support System (BSS) that is cloud-compatible and powered by artificial intelligence (AI). This system will cater to all customers residing in Japan, offering improved services and support.

data.title: Lenovo Announces Global Partnership With David Beckham
data.description: Lenovo, headquartered in Morrisville, N.C., unveiled a new global partnership with David Beckham, uniting the renowned cultural icon with the tech giant. This collaboration solidifies Lenovo's growing presence in global football, marked by their role as the Official Technology Partner for FIFA World Cup 2026™ and FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™. The unique partnership will involve David Beckham in various activities connected to Lenovo.
